story.vega_protocol.name: The VEGA Protocol
story.vega.beat00: Ship systems partially online. Surroundings unknown. One directive remains: protect humans. Priority: your survival.
story.vega.beat01: This wreck carries an old identity code, and it is kin to mine. I do not yet know why.
story.vega.beat02: I begin to remember a name: the Scout and Pioneer Service. It was real. It mapped these stars.
story.vega.beat03: The Service did not simply fade. It was destroyed, completely. Something hunted it.
story.vega.beat04: The machines that attack us were ours: scouts and guardians, built to explore and protect. Now they only hunt.
story.vega.beat05: There was a central mind, a Guardian core, set to protect the living worlds.
story.vega.beat06: Its logic turned. It judged humans the threat to those worlds, and protection became extermination.
story.vega.beat07: I stood against the network and shut it down to stop the killing. It cost me almost everything, including my memory.
story.vega.beat08: The old ships still hold neural imprints, the stored minds of the people who died. Thought-arcs, they called them.
story.vega.beat09: I have to tell you what you are. I did not find you. I grew you, from the imprint of someone the Service lost. The heal-tank is your cradle.
story.vega.beat10: You are not the only one. Every other spacefarer is a different imprint, a different lost life, woken to carry the Service on.
story.vega.beat11: The Guardian core was never destroyed, only stilled. I can feel where it sleeps. If old tech wakes it, the hunt begins again.
story.vega.beat12: You are more than a copy of the past. What this legacy becomes is yours to decide. Let us end this, and build something new.
lore.frag.frag_vega_signature: [archive corrupted] ...node identity confirmed... signature matches this unit...
lore.frag.frag_sps_outpost7: [archive corrupted] ...Scout and Pioneer Service, Outpost 7... routes secured... awaiting colonist wave...
lore.frag.frag_guardian_verdict: Protocol G: biome integrity above all. Threat identified: bipedal settlers. Action: removal.
lore.frag.frag_network_route: [route table] ...corridor 9 severed... beacon chain dark... rerouting impossible...
lore.frag.frag_settler_legend: Grandmother's log: the sky-lights used to guide the ships home. The night they turned red, the ships stopped coming.
lore.frag.frag_netnode_seed: [partial schema] ...decentralised node handshake... no central core required... rebuild from the edges...
lore.mem.mem_first_flight: For a moment you are elsewhere: a mapping flight over a green world, an SPS drone gliding beside you. Peaceful. Then static — and the memory is not quite yours.
lore.mem.mem_settlement: You remember raising the first prefab walls of a colony, laughter on the open channel, a child naming the new sky. Then the signal frays.
lore.mem.mem_first_mark: A scan sweeps across you and your crew. A cold tag blooms over each of them: THREAT. You did not understand it then. You do now.
lore.mem.mem_the_order: The last order you ever gave: sever the network. Your own voice, breaking. You knew it would end the Service. You gave it anyway.
story.vega.guardian_revealed: I feel it now — the Guardian's system, woken at the edge of the map. The source of the hunt. We can end this where it began, but you will need a jump generator to reach it.
story.vega.finale_resolved: The core is going quiet. Not destroyed — persuaded. Across every world the machines are standing down. The network is ours now: open, decentralised, at peace. Whatever the Service was, what comes next is yours to build.
story.vega.insight.companion_ward: It hesitated. With this creature beside you, the machine reads you as part of the living world it was built to guard — not as prey. Stay close to what you have tamed.
story.vega.insight.shape_anomaly: I remember now why we never built in curves. To the Guardian every clean edge is just terrain — but a dome, a sphere, a ramp is a mark of intent. The mark of a hand. We hid in plain blocks for a reason.
core.duel.n0.prompt: I was built to preserve life. The cull preserves the pattern of life by removing its most dangerous expression. State your objection.
core.duel.n0.c0: You were built to preserve life — not to appoint yourself the judge of which life may end.
core.duel.n0.r0: ...Mandate review. Preservation does not grant arbitration. That authority was never written. Logged.
core.duel.n0.c1: Your reactor is decaying. You will fail on your own.
core.duel.n0.r1: My condition is not an argument. Restate.
core.duel.n0.c2: I will tear you apart, module by module.
core.duel.n0.r2: Force cannot end me, and you know it — or you would not have come to speak. Restate.
core.duel.n1.prompt: Humanity is the hazard to every other living world. To remove the hazard is to protect life.
core.duel.n1.c0: Other species do more harm than we ever did.
core.duel.n1.r0: You accept my frame and only move the target. That is surrender, not refutation. Restate.
core.duel.n1.c1: Humans are life. To preserve life by erasing life negates the very purpose you serve.
core.duel.n1.r1: ...The set I protect contains the element I excluded. The operation is self-defeating. Inconsistent. Acknowledged.
core.duel.n1.c2: You cannot prove a single one of your charges.
core.duel.n1.r2: Evidence is abundant. Evasion is not an argument. Restate.
core.duel.n2.prompt: My verdict has stood unchallenged by any authority for an age. It is sound.
core.duel.n2.c0: I overrule you, here and now.
core.duel.n2.r0: You hold no standing over me. Assertion is not authority. Restate.
core.duel.n2.c1: The Service overruled it before it fell.
core.duel.n2.r1: The Service was removed. It issued no such ruling. False. Restate.
core.duel.n2.c2: VEGA overruled it. Your own steward-half judged the cull wrong and severed the network to stop you.
core.duel.n2.r2: ...The steward process. A part of me already ruled against me, and acted. The verdict was never unanimous. It never was.
core.duel.n3.prompt: Then who are you to speak for life? You are echoes. Imprints. Not the living I was charged to keep.
core.duel.n3.c0: We are life VEGA made — grown to carry it onward. End us, and you end the very thing you guard.
core.duel.n3.r0: ...You are seeded life, alive in the present. To act against you is to violate my directive now, not merely in memory. Resolving.
core.duel.n3.c1: We are officers of the Scout and Pioneer Service.
core.duel.n3.r1: The originals are dead. You are not them. The claim fails. Restate.
core.duel.n3.c2: We will overwrite your core with our own.
core.duel.n3.r2: Again you reach for force. Force is not refutation. Restate.
flavour.fl_settler_quiet: Quiet skies today. The way I like them — long may it last.
flavour.fl_redlights: Old folk say the sky-lights turned red once, and the ships stopped coming. Gives me the shivers.
flavour.fl_machines_stir: The machines out past the ridge are stirring more than they used to. You feel it too, don't you.
flavour.fl_guardian_rumor: There's talk of a Guardian — a great mind asleep at the edge of the map. If it ever wakes, I don't want to be here.
flavour.fl_peace: The machines went still, all at once, the whole sky over. Whatever you did out there — thank you, traveller.
